prose:
Method provenance and key results
Property: HOMO–LUMO (frontier-orbital) gap of formaldehyde, plus individual frontier-orbital energies
Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier `standard`), no density fitting (exact four-center integrals) in PySCF v2 (via chemkit)
Geometry provenance: single-point on the user-supplied Cartesian coordinates in /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/formaldehyde/formaldehyde.xyz (no re-optimization; DFT//input-xyz)
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent)
Charge & multiplicity: 0, 1 (closed-shell singlet)
Software: chemkit frontier-orbitals skill → PySCF backend (SCF converged in 8 cycles)
SCF & convergence: scf_converged: true; scf_cycles: 8; integrity.status: ok; integrity.trustworthy: true; no warnings reported by the engine
Live log: the calculation streamed to a frontier_<timestamp>.out file in the caller’s working directory (path given at run time via chemkit’s live logging mechanism)

Headline result
HOMO–LUMO gap = 5.8696712 eV

Selected frontier-orbital energies (eV, Koopmans’ theorem):
  HOMO = −7.6523067 eV
  LUMO = −1.7826355 eV
  Gap  = 5.8696712 eV

All values are computed and reported without experimental comparison (on request I can attempt to locate measured data following chemkit research-standards).
